figures.caption: Fig. 1. Top: Task plans with 12 robots coordinating to track 4 dynamic targets across 12 tasks in two scenes (Scene-1: left and middle; Scene2: right). Middle: ROS simulation with 8 robots and 3 dynamic targets executing 10 tasks. Bottom: Hardware experiments with 4 robots and 2 dynamic targets performing 7 tasks, showing snapshots at different times.

Fig. 2. Overview of the proposed framework, consisting of four main components: (i) trajectory estimation via LSTM and CP, (ii) task decomposition into an R-poset, (iii) CP-MCTS for uncertainty-aware assignment, and (iv) online execution and receding-horizon adaptation. In the R-poset illustration, precedence and mutual-exclusion relations are marked by black and red arrows, respectively.
Fig. 5. ROS simulation results. Top: Robot and target trajectories. Bottom: Gantt chart of the execution timeline with task allocation and replanning.

Fig. 6. Hardware experiment results. Left: Recorded robot and target trajectories. Top-right: Example trajectory snapshots highlighting robottarget interactions. Bottom-right: Gantt chart of the execution timeline.
